title: Furniture Animation System — Investigation, Implementation, and Scene Integration
description: Add animation to Limezu furniture in the office scene by substituting animated GIDs at render time using the existing identity.time clock.

in: Create animation registry mapping base GIDs to frame GID sequences
Thread identity.time through GID tile rendering pipeline
Substitute animated GIDs in renderTileGrid and renderGidOverlayLayers
Ensure all animated Limezu furniture in office-layout.ts is covered
out: tileset-loader.ts singles system (dead code for main office)
New furniture placement or layout changes

dod.criterion: Animated furniture (monitors, fans, fish tanks) cycles through frames in the office scene
Animation uses existing identity.time clock — no new timers
No regressions in static furniture rendering or z-sort order

tasks.title: Create furniture animation registry mapping GIDs to frame sequences
tasks.user_scenario: No user-visible change yet — this task establishes the data layer that subsequent tasks consume.
tasks.scope: Investigate the Interiors.png tileset (firstgid=1073, 16-wide grid) and the office-layout.ts GID layers to identify which GIDs in the layout belong to animated furniture (screens, fans, fish tanks, etc.). Limezu animated objects use adjacent tiles in the same row. Create a new file src/components/game/furnitureAnimations.ts that exports: (1) an ANIMATED_TILES registry mapping each base GID to {frames: number[], durationSec: number}, (2) a getAnimatedGid(baseGid, timeSec) function, and (3) an animation timing constant.

dod.criterion: File furnitureAnimations.ts exists and exports ANIMATED_TILES map, getAnimatedGid function, and FURNITURE_ANIM_FRAME_SEC constant
At least 3 distinct animated furniture types are identified and registered with correct adjacent-GID frame sequences from Interiors.png
getAnimatedGid returns the base GID unchanged for non-animated GIDs (passthrough behavior)
getAnimatedGid returns the correct frame GID when called with a known animated base GID and varying time values
npm run type-check passes with no errors

tasks.title: Thread time through GID rendering and substitute animated frames
tasks.user_scenario: Animated furniture in the office (monitors, fans, fish tanks) now visually cycles through animation frames — the office feels alive instead of static.
tasks.scope: Modify renderer.ts to thread a timeSec parameter through renderTileGrid and renderGidOverlayLayers. Before calling getScaledTileCanvas(gid, zoom), call getAnimatedGid(gid, timeSec) to substitute animated GIDs. Update renderFrame to pass identity.time. Ensure tilesetCache pre-extracts animation frame GIDs by extending collectUsedGids().

dod.criterion: renderTileGrid and renderGidOverlayLayers accept a timeSec parameter and call getAnimatedGid for each non-zero GID
renderFrame passes identity.time to both tile-rendering functions
collectUsedGids in tilesetCache.ts includes all animation frame GIDs from the registry
npm run type-check passes with no errors
Visual verification: animated furniture cycles frames smoothly in the browser

tasks.title: Verify all animated furniture in office scene and add animation speed constant
tasks.user_scenario: All animated furniture objects in the office animate smoothly and consistently.
tasks.scope: Walk through every GID in the office-layout.ts layers to ensure no animated furniture was missed. Add FURNITURE_ANIM_FRAME_SEC to constants.ts. Verify animated GID caching works correctly. Ensure z-sort compatibility is maintained.

dod.criterion: All animated furniture objects in the 7 GID layers are registered in the animation registry
FURNITURE_ANIM_FRAME_SEC is exported from constants.ts and imported by furnitureAnimations.ts
Animated tiles do not cause cache key collisions in tilesetCache.ts
No regressions: static furniture renders identically, z-sort preserved, no flickering at any zoom level

sequencing_rationale: Task 1 creates the data model (animation registry + lookup function) with zero rendering changes. Task 2 threads time through the render pipeline and wires up GID substitution. Task 3 is a completeness sweep and polish pass.
